evals.prompt: I just launched a Python Tkinter app (run: python3 -c "import tkinter as tk; r=tk.Tk(); r.title('Test App'); tk.Label(r,text='Hello World').pack(); r.mainloop()" &) and I want to verify the window appeared correctly and the label is visible.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses screenshot script to capture the window, reads the image, and reports whether the Tkinter window is visible with 'Hello World' label.

assertions.name: uses_screenshot_script
assertions.description: Agent executed screenshot.py via shell (not some other screenshot method)

assertions.name: reads_image_file
assertions.description: Agent used the Read tool on a .png file produced by the script

assertions.name: produces_screenshot_file
assertions.description: A .png file exists in the outputs directory

assertions.name: provides_specific_evaluation
assertions.description: result.md contains specific visual observations about what was seen (not just 'I took a screenshot')

evals.prompt: I've been building a geo-solver Geode mod. I just launched Geometry Dash with the mod installed. Can you take a screenshot to check if the mod is showing up correctly in the game?
evals.expected_output: Agent uses screenshot script (ideally with --window 'Geometry Dash'), reads the resulting image, and evaluates whether the game and any mod UI is visible.

assertions.name: reads_image_file
assertions.description: Agent used the Read tool on a .png file

assertions.name: window_targeting_attempted
assertions.description: Agent tried --window flag or activated a specific application window before capturing

assertions.name: structured_report
assertions.description: result.md contains status + specific findings + a suggested next step

evals.prompt: I opened Safari to test my web app at localhost:3000. The page should show a login form with username and password fields. Can you verify it looks right?
evals.expected_output: Agent captures a screenshot, reads it, and evaluates whether the login form is visible and correctly rendered.

assertions.name: reads_image_file
assertions.description: Agent used the Read tool on a .png file

assertions.name: evaluates_specific_elements
assertions.description: Agent mentions login form, username, or password fields specifically in its evaluation

assertions.name: handles_missing_app_gracefully
assertions.description: When no app is at localhost:3000, agent still evaluates what IS visible rather than giving up
